<!-- Generated by spike-init on 2026-05-13. This is a prose-only repo (Markdown + HTML)
     with no build/test/lint tooling — there were no commands to execute, so nothing
     here carries a "✅ verified" stamp. Re-run /spike-init to refresh. -->

# spikekit — agent context

spikekit is a set of Claude Code **skills** for "figuring out what to build and how":
`/spike` (run throwaway experiments to de-risk an approach), `/spike-wrap` (consolidate a
finished spike into design docs), `/spike-init` (research a repo and write its
`AGENTS.md`/`CLAUDE.md`). It is a **content repo** — Markdown and HTML, no source code.

## Project layout

- `skills/<name>/SKILL.md` — one skill per directory. YAML frontmatter (`name`,
  `description`) + a Markdown body that *is* the skill's instructions.
  - `skills/<name>/assets/` — optional templates/examples a skill starts from or copies
    (e.g. `skills/spike-wrap/assets/summary.html`, `skills/spike-wrap/assets/index.html`).
- `docs/designs/` — durable design records, produced by `/spike-wrap`:
  - `docs/designs/README.md` — the index table (Topic · Title · Status · Date · Gist ·
    agent doc · human doc).
  - `docs/designs/agents/<topic>/design.md` — the detailed, agent-facing doc; **the
    source of truth** an implementer builds from. An `assets/` dir alongside it holds
    diagrams, screenshots, sample files.
  - `docs/designs/humans/<topic>/index.html` — a short, skimmable, self-contained HTML
    one-pager (inline CSS) for humans.
  - `docs/designs/humans/index.html` — browsable index of the human one-pagers.
- `.spike/<topic>/` — scratch space for an in-progress spike. **Gitignored, disposable;
  `/spike-wrap` offers to delete it once consolidated — the user decides.** Never commit
  it; don't reference it from committed docs.
- `README.md` — project overview + install instructions.

## Environment setup

Nothing to set up — no package manager, no lockfile, no dependencies, no build step.
Clone and edit Markdown/HTML directly.

To *use* the skills in Claude Code, symlink them into `~/.claude/skills/` (see `README.md`):

```sh
ln -s "$PWD/skills/spike"      ~/.claude/skills/spike
ln -s "$PWD/skills/spike-wrap" ~/.claude/skills/spike-wrap
ln -s "$PWD/skills/spike-init" ~/.claude/skills/spike-init
```

## Tests / lint / type-check / build

None — there is no test suite, linter, type checker, or build system in this repo, and
no CI. The real "test" of a skill is invoking it (`/spike`, `/spike-wrap`, `/spike-init`)
in Claude Code and watching it behave; the real test of a design doc is whether a fresh
agent can implement from it.

## Conventions

**Skills (`skills/<name>/SKILL.md`):**
- `name:` is kebab-case and matches the directory name; it's also the slash command
  (`/spike` → `skills/spike/SKILL.md`).
- `description:` is the *triggering* text — third-person ("Use this when…"), packed with
  the situations and phrasings that should fire it, with `/<name>` mentioned. It's what
  Claude matches on; the body is only read after the skill is invoked.
- The body is plain Markdown — numbered steps, imperative voice.
- These skills are authored *in English* but instruct the agent to converse with the user
  and write user-facing prose **in the user's working language**, while keeping
  machine-facing bits as-is (import lines like `@AGENTS.md`, command names, the
  `✅ verified <date>` marker, provenance HTML-comment blocks). Follow that pattern in any
  new or edited skill.

**Design records:** always a *pair* — the detailed agent Markdown
(`docs/designs/agents/<topic>/design.md`, the source of truth) and the short human HTML
(`docs/designs/humans/<topic>/index.html`). When you add or change one, update the other,
the `docs/designs/README.md` index row, and `docs/designs/humans/index.html`. Human HTML
pages are self-contained (inline CSS, no external assets).

## Notes

This repo is small and prose-only. If it ever grows tooling — an HTML linter, a
link-checker for the design index, a markdown formatter — add the commands to this file
and re-run `/spike-init` so they get executed and stamped `✅ verified`.
